title: artocarpus horridus
intro: Artocarpus horridus, also known as the stinking nut or kwansaba, is a plant species native to tropical Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain compounds with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The seeds have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, offering potential health benefits such as immune system support, anti-aging, and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the seeds are often used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap the plant's medicinal benefits.
